Implementation Action: Implement the registration code delivery workflow in EEEEE so the code is sent only via the approved out-of-band channel to the recorded address of record
Action Detail: Use the user’s physical or digital address held in authoritative records, not a self-asserted address, and ensure the code is delivered separately from the registration session.

Implementation Action: Record evidence in EEEEE showing successful out-of-band delivery of the registration code for identity proofing cases
Action Detail: Retain records that demonstrate the code was delivered to the address of record and used to verify the user during registration.

Implementation Action: Review EEEEE identity and authentication procedures to confirm they accept externally proofed identities at NIST SP 800-63B Level 3
Action Detail: Ensure the application’s onboarding and authentication process explicitly accepts identities proofed by an external agency or organisation at the required assurance level.

Implementation Action: Update EEEEE system security plan to document acceptance of externally proofed identities at NIST SP 800-63B Level 3
Action Detail: Record the identity assurance level, the external proofing basis, and how the application relies on enterprise identity services to accept those identities.

Implementation Action: Record evidence in EEEEE compliance artefacts showing externally proofed identities are accepted at NIST SP 800-63B Level 3
Action Detail: Retain the relevant procedure, system security plan entry, and any operational records demonstrating the application accepts externally proofed identities as required.

Implementation Action: Document how EEEEE uses enterprise identity providers and authorisation servers for user, device and NPE authentication and authorisation decisions
Action Detail: Record the enterprise SSO/federation services and token/assertion mechanisms relied upon by EEEEE, and map them to the control requirement and the organisation’s authentication and authorisation policy.

Implementation Action: Update the EEEEE system security plan and system design documentation to describe identity provider and authorisation server integration
Action Detail: Include the identities managed, the attributes and access rights used, and the SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, X.509 device certificate and signed JWT access token flows used by the application.

Implementation Action: Configure EEEEE to use the approved enterprise identity provider and authorisation server mechanisms for authentication and access decisions
Action Detail: Implement the approved federation and token-based integration points only; do not create local identity stores where enterprise services are intended to provide identity and access management.

Implementation Action: Verify and record that EEEEE’s identity provider and authorisation server integrations operate as documented
Action Detail: Confirm the application is using the approved mechanisms for user, device and NPE identity, attributes and access rights, and retain configuration and test evidence for assessment.

Implementation Action: Locate the enterprise identification and authentication policy and document how EEEEE complies with the requirements for cryptographic keys that protect access tokens
Action Detail: Record the application-specific obligations for key generation, management, protection from disclosure, and protection from misuse within EEEEE's operating procedures and security documentation.

Implementation Action: Document EEEEE's cryptographic key establishment and management procedure for access-token signing keys
Action Detail: Define how signing keys are generated, stored, rotated, revoked, backed up where applicable, and retired for the application, using the enterprise-approved cryptographic process and modules.

Implementation Action: Update EEEEE system design documentation and system security plan to describe the cryptographic mechanisms used to sign access tokens and protect the private keys
Action Detail: Capture the key lifecycle, the cryptographic module or service used, and the protections applied to the private keys in the application boundary and supporting environment.

Implementation Action: Implement the approved configuration for EEEEE so that access-token signing keys are generated and stored only in the enterprise-approved cryptographic module or managed key service
Action Detail: Apply the required application configuration and environment settings so private signing keys are not exposed in source code, application files, or other non-approved storage locations.

Implementation Action: Document and retain evidence that EEEEE access-token signing keys are protected from disclosure and misuse
Action Detail: Keep configuration records, key-management records, and operational evidence showing who can access the keys, how access is restricted, and how key use is controlled within the application environment.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Review EEEEE’s system security plan and identity and access management design to confirm how identity assertions and access tokens are verified before access is granted
Action Detail: Document the application’s use of enterprise identity provider mechanisms, including where signature and metadata verification occurs for connected networks, applications, and APIs.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Document EEEEE’s configuration settings and supporting evidence showing that identity assertions and access tokens are verified before granting access
Action Detail: Capture the relevant application, federation, or API gateway settings and retain the associated configuration records as assessment evidence.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Verify that EEEEE only accepts identity assertions and access tokens from approved enterprise identity provider mechanisms
Action Detail: Check the application’s integration points and access paths to confirm the source of assertions and tokens is validated before access is allowed.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Verify that EEEEE checks the digital signature integrity of identity assertions and access tokens before access is granted
Action Detail: Confirm the application or its integrated identity services reject unsigned or tampered assertions and tokens in line with the control requirement.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Verify that EEEEE validates required metadata in identity assertions and access tokens before access is granted
Action Detail: Confirm the application checks access-request metadata such as user, resource, transaction, and time-related fields where applicable to the access flow.

Control Title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
Implementation Action: Retain assessment records demonstrating that EEEEE verifies the source and integrity of identity assertions and access tokens before access is granted
Action Detail: Keep the reviewed design, configuration, and operational evidence together for audit and assessment use.

Implementation Action: Document EEEEE’s token management procedure covering generation, issuance, refresh, revocation, time restriction and audience restriction for access tokens and assertions
Action Detail: Align the procedure to the enterprise identification and authentication policy, access control policy, and the control requirement for token lifecycle management.

Implementation Action: Confirm that EEEEE’s system design documentation records how access tokens and assertions are generated, issued, refreshed, revoked, time-restricted and audience-restricted
Action Detail: Update the application design records to show the approved token flows and the controls used to enforce validity lifetime and audience scope.

Implementation Action: Configure EEEEE’s token settings to enforce the approved token lifetime and audience restrictions
Action Detail: Apply the required validity period and audience scope in the application or its integrated identity/token handling components.

Implementation Action: Verify that EEEEE can revoke or invalidate access tokens and assertions when they are compromised, lost, or no longer needed
Action Detail: Test the operational revocation path and retain evidence that invalidation works as required by the control.

Implementation Action: Collect evidence showing EEEEE issues, refreshes, and revokes access tokens and assertions in accordance with the approved procedure
Action Detail: Retain configuration extracts, procedure references, and test records demonstrating compliance with the control objectives.
